QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_33

question.text: What is the minimum annuity percentage required for premature exit from NPS?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For premature exit from NPS, a minimum of 80% of the corpus must be used to purchase an annuity. Only 20% can be withdrawn as lump sum. **The Trap:** Normal exit requires 40% annuity. Premature exit doubles this to 80%. The higher annuity requirement discourages early exits. **Cross-Exam Context:** Premature exit also requires a minimum of 10 years of NPS subscription. If corpus is Rs.2.5 lakh or less, entire lump sum is allowed. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Counsel clients against premature exit by highlighting the 80% annuity lock-in. The 20% lump sum may not serve their liquidity needs.
